title: Housing Advocacy Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Advocacy Groups 5 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF COMMUNITY ALLIANCE OF TENANTS RENTERS' RIGHTS HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a renters' rights hotline in Oregon. Topics include fair housing protections, evictions, terminations, repair concerns, security deposits, and rent increase limits, etc. Does not provide information for landlords. Priority is given to renters in Southern and Central Oregon, Mid-Valley, and Portland/Tri-County area. Residents of all other Oregon regions strongly encouraged to become members to receive prioritized assistance. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Hotline intake and callbacks: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday 1pm-5pm, Tuesday 5pm-8pm Member's Live Hotline: Tuesday 6pm-7pm, Saturday 1pm-2pm Email (503) 288-0130 Visit Website More Details FAIR HOUSING COUNCIL OF OREGON HOUSING DISCRIMINATION ASSISTANCE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Promotes equal access to housing by providing education and outreach services explaining fair housing law. Investigates and provides enforcement assistance following fair housing complaints involving discrimination when acquiring or retaining housing. Depending on the results, can make a referral to an attorney, the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), or a state agency. Does not field complaints directly, but can provide assistance with illegal discrimination in any housing setting, including shelters. Fair housing laws are federal, state, and local laws that prohibit housing discrimination based on "protected classes." Protected classes include race, color, national origin, religion, gender, families with children, and disability at the federal level, as well as additional protections at state and local levels. The laws cover rental, sales, lending, and insurance transactions. Illegal actions include discrimination through denial of housing, refusal to negotiate, providing false information, eviction, selectively applying rules or standards, advertisements or statements, harassment, intimidation, provision of services, or steering. 1221 SW Yamhill Street Suite 305 Portland, OR 972052110 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (800) 424-3247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITE OREGON CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects and expands the rights of immigrant, refugee, and underserved communities by offering community organizing, policy advocacy, community education, and leadership development. Promotes systemic changes in many issues including affordable housing, fair wages, racial justice, etc. by empowering communities and grassroots organizing. Does not provide direct advocacy for individuals. 1390 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Immigrant and refugee community members and non-immigrant community members Hours: Varies Email (503) 287-4117 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITE OREGON UNITE OREGON ROGUE VALLEY CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects and expands the rights of immigrant, refugee, and underserved communities by offering community organizing, policy advocacy, community education, and leadership development.
